title: Production and regeneration of G. lucidum protoplasts
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ip5gd","text":"Production and regeneration of G. lucidum protoplasts, which is important to G.lucidum monokaryotization","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"scskv","text":"G.lucidum have two different types hypha in mycelium--the haploid one and the diploid one. Using a protoplast regeneration method can generate an isolate from a single cell which maybe only contain a suit of chromsomes. Firstly, we digest the mycelium into a single protoplast. Then, dilute the protoplast  suspension into an appropriate concentration. Third, regenerate these protoplasts on MYG medium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inoculate mycelia into 100 ml PDB media (200 g potato, 20 g dextrose, 1 L water) in 250 ml flasks.
Cultivate the mycelia were cultivated in the dark at 28°C under continuous shaking at 50 rpm.
Harvest the cultures by filtering through sterile gauze.
Place approximately 5 g of mycelia in a 50 ml tube containing 20 ml of filter-sterilized 1.5% lywallzyme (Guangdong Institute of Microbiology, China) in 0.6 M mannitol.
Incubate the mixture at 30°C for approximately 2 h with occasional agitation.
Remove hyphae fragments after 2 h of digestion. MYG agar medium (2% malt extract, 0.2% yeast extract, 2% dextrose, 1.5% agar) was used for G. lucidum protoplast regeneration.
Dilute the protoplast suspension was diluted to 1 x 10 4 cells/ml, and 50 µl of the diluted protoplast suspension was added to a MYG medium plate and inoculated at 25°C in the dark.
creator: Protocols Importer
